description_long: This apartment building features a three-story structure with a gray stone exterior and a steeply pitched, shingled roof. The facade is punctuated by regularly spaced, rectangular windows with orange-brown frames, giving the building a uniform and orderly appearance. The roof includes dormer windows and visible weathering, adding character and a sense of age to the structure. A small covered entrance is present on one side, providing access to the interior. The building's massing is slightly asymmetrical, with a protruding section on one side and a stepped roofline. The overall design suggests a utilitarian yet classic urban residential building, likely found in older city neighborhoods.
description: A three-story gray stone apartment building with a steep shingled roof, regular orange-framed windows, and a small covered entrance.
description_view_0: Side view showing three floors, six windows, and the main roof with visible shingles and weathering.
description_view_1: Narrow end view with a single window on the upper floor, a prominent roof peak, and the main entrance at ground level.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with four windows per floor, a dormer window on the roof, and a protruding lower section.
description_view_3: Rear view with a symmetrical arrangement of windows and a simple, unadorned facade.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are arranged in a regular, symmetrical grid pattern with even spacing both vertically and horizontally across each facade.
